double_space_ellipsis.key:
Women are obligated in Kriat Shema al HaMitah (קְרִיאַת שְׁמַע עַל הַמִּטָּה — the Bedtime Shema), even though women are generally exempt from most time-bound positive commandments (mitzvot aseh shehazman graman, like the daytime Shema at a specific hour). Bedtime Shema is treated differently for several reasons:

Spiritual protection (shmirah — שְׁמִירָה):
The Bedtime Shema is not only a timed recitation. The Talmud teaches that these words guard a person while the soul is partially withdrawn during sleep. Women need this protection during sleep just as men do.

Hamapil (הַמַּפִּיל):
The blessing recited just before sleep — "Who casts the bands of sleep upon my eyes..." — is a blessing of praise and a request for protection. Many women say it with the full bedtime order; Ashkenazi custom is to recite it when you lie down intending to sleep (Mishnah Berurah 239:6). If you are genuinely unsure you will sleep soon — per Sephardi and kabbalistic guidance — you may omit the closing Baruch Atah… or say the words as meditation without G-d's name (see the Hamapil checklist item).

The core verse:
Even beyond the full bedtime order, reciting the first verse of Shema (Shema Yisrael) is an acknowledgment of G-d's sovereignty that applies to everyone.

What to say:
• At minimum: the first paragraph of Shema ("Shema Yisrael..." through "uvshochbecha uvkumecha")
• Recommended: all three paragraphs of Shema, as printed in your siddur's bedtime section
• Then Hamapil, as the last blessing before closing your eyes
• Many siddurim also include psalms (such as Psalm 91) and brief prayers of trust in G-d

Customs:
Some traditions omit certain prayers in the full bedtime order (for example, some are cautious about saying Hamapil if they are unsure they will fall asleep immediately). The core Shema and the protective blessings remain universal for women. Ask your rabbi or rebbetzin about your community's practice.

Timing:
Say it right before getting into bed to go to sleep for the night — at your actual bedtime, not when taking a short daytime nap. If you go to bed later than halachic midnight, say it then. If you forgot and have not yet slept, say it when you remember.

Distinct from Maariv:
If you pray Maariv or say the evening Shema separately, the Bedtime Shema at sleep is still its own mitzvah — different time and purpose (protection through the night).

Use a siddur or the guides below for the exact text step by step.

double_space_ellipsis.key:
The rabbis instituted a practice of saying 100 blessings (brachot) every day, keeping us perpetually aware of G-d's gifts.

Who is obligated:
The Talmudic practice was framed for men (Menachot 43b). Many poskim hold that women should also aim for 100 brachot daily; others treat it as optional for women — ask your rav.

What a bracha is:
A bracha (בְּרָכָה — blessing, plural: brachot) is a short formula said before or after performing a mitzvah or enjoying something from the world. Every bracha begins: "Baruch Atah Ado-nai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam..." — Blessed are You, L-rd our G-d, King of the universe...

Where the 100 come from on a weekday:
• Morning blessings (Birchot HaShachar): ~15
• Morning prayer service (Shacharit): ~40
• Afternoon prayer (Mincha): ~20
• Evening prayer (Maariv): ~18
• Blessings before and after food: ~10
Total: easily 100 on a regular weekday

On Shabbat and Yom Tov (Jewish holidays):
The prayer services are shorter and the Amidah has fewer blessings, so you will fall short of 100. To make up the count:
• Eat additional snacks and say brachot before and after each
• Smell fragrant spices or fruit and say the blessing on fragrances (besamim — בְּשָׂמִים)

Ein Kelokeinu (Ashkenaz custom on Shabbat & Yom Tov):
Ashkenazim worldwide recite Ein Kelokeinu (אֵין כֵאלֹקֵינו) at the end of Shacharit on Shabbat and festivals — in Israel and the Diaspora alike. (On weekdays, Ashkenazim omit it; Sephardim and Chabad recite it daily.) Ashkenaz recite it on Shabbat and Yom Tov especially because the weekday Amidah has nineteen blessings while the Shabbat Amidah has only seven, so you fall further short of one hundred on those days. Following Rashi (Menachot 43b), some rabbis hold that the praises in this hymn count toward the daily hundred when you are lacking — not as full brachot with Shem Umalchus (G-d's name and kingship in the usual formula), but as intentional recognition of G-d. The Taz (OC 642) cites this as a reason for the custom. Many count twenty lines (five stanzas of four); some count twenty-one when the opening line is repeated at the end, as in many siddurim. An older reckoning (Shibbolei Haleket) counts twelve, parallel to the twelve weekday Amidah blessings omitted on Shabbat. Note on Yom Kippur: Unlike the minor fasts, Ein Kelokeinu is universally chanted at the conclusion of the Musaf service (or Ne'ilah) on Yom Kippur across mainstream Ashkenazic, Sephardic, and Chassidic traditions. The Mishnah Berurah prefers making up the count with real brachot — food, fragrances, or answering Amen to aliyot — when you can, rather than relying only on these praises.

Tzaddik (צדיק) — a framework some rabbis recommend:
Some teachers suggest using the four letters of the word Tzaddik as a daily checklist alongside the 100 brachot:
• צ (Tzadi = 90): Answer 90 Amens each day (responding "Amen" when you hear blessings in prayer or from others).
• ד (Dalet = 4): Respond to the 4 Kedushot (קְדֻשּׁוֹת — sanctification prayers) in the daily services: (1) Kedushah in Yotzer Or (the blessing before the morning Shema), (2) Kedushah in the Shacharit Amidah repetition, (3) Kedushah D'Sidra / Uva L'Tzion (recited at the end of Shacharit), and (4) Kedushah in the Mincha Amidah repetition.
• י (Yud = 10): Say 10 Amens to "Yehei Shemei Rabbah" (יְהֵא שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא) during Kaddish throughout the day.
• ק (Kuf = 100): Recite 100 brachot (blessings) each day — the obligation described above.

es.snippet: YaKNeHaZ es un mnemónico para el orden cuando Shabat pasa directamente a Yom Tov (por ejemplo, noche de Pesaj). Usa la sección de Kiddush festivo nocturno de tu sidur.

es.key: Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) is the blessing on grain foods that are not bread — cake, crackers, pasta, cereal. It is shorter than hamotzi but still thanks G-d for grain sustenance.
es.snippet: Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) es la bendición sobre alimentos de grano que no son pan — pastel, galletas, pasta, cereal. Es más corta que hamotzi pero agradece a D-s por el sustento de grano.

es.key: Next time you have guests, keep in mind to escort them on their way as they leave, even for just a few steps. The Talmud (Sotah 46b) says the reward for escorting others has no measure!
es.snippet: La próxima vez que tengas invitados, recuerda escoltarlos al salir, aunque sean solo unos pasos. El Talmud (Sotah 46b) dice que la recompensa por acompañar a otros no tiene medida.

es.key: Ruach Ra'ah ("evil spirit") is a rabbinic term for the impurity resting on hands after sleep. Morning netilat yadayim removes it by washing hands alternating right and left 3 times each.
es.snippet: Ruach Ra'ah ("espíritu malo") es un término rabínico para la impureza en las manos tras dormir. El netilat yadayim matutino lo elimina lavando manos alternando derecha e izquierda 3 veces cada una.

es.key: Asara B'Tevet — minor fast on 10 Tevet from dawn until nightfall; the only fast never postponed when it falls on Friday before Shabbat — in which case we break it with Friday night Kiddush
es.snippet: Asara B'Tevet — ayuno menor el 10 de Tevet desde el amanecer hasta la noche; el único ayuno que nunca se pospone cuando cae en viernes antes de Shabat — en cuyo caso lo rompemos con el Kiddush del viernes por la noche

es.key: Kallah means bride; kallah classes teach taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot of the home, and wedding laws before marriage. The kallah and chatan prepare for chuppah and building a Jewish family.
es.snippet: Kallah significa novia; las clases de kallah enseñan taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot del hogar y leyes de boda antes del matrimonio. La kallah y el chatan se preparan para la jupá y construir una familia judía.

es.key: Vatodi'enu ("You have made us know") is an insert in the Maariv Amidah on Saturday night when Yom Tov begins after Shabbat. It acknowledges that Shabbat has ended and the festival has begun.
es.snippet: Vatodi'enu ("Nos has hecho saber") es una inserción en la Amidá de Maariv el sábado por la noche cuando Yom Tov comienza tras Shabat. Reconoce que Shabat terminó y la festividad comenzó.

es.key: Mezonot — Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) is the blessing on grain foods that are not bread — cake, crackers, pasta, cereal. It is shorter than hamotzi but still thanks G-d for grain sustenance.
es.snippet: Mezonot — Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) es la bendición sobre alimentos de grano que no son pan — pastel, galletas, pasta, cereal. Es más corta que hamotzi pero agradece a D-s por el sustento de grano.

es.key: Yaknehaz — YaKNeHaZ is a mnemonic for the order when Shabbat flows directly into Yom Tov (for example Saturday night Pesach). Use your siddur's festival night Kiddush section for instructions.
es.snippet: Yaknehaz — YaKNeHaZ es un mnemónico para el orden cuando Shabat pasa directamente a Yom Tov (por ejemplo, noche de Pesaj). Usa la sección de Kiddush festivo nocturno de tu sidur.

es.key: Purim is tomorrow. Plan all four mitzvot: Megillah (night and morning), matanot la'evyonim, mishloach manot, and tomorrow's festive seudah (afternoon meal). Confirm reading times with your shul.
es.snippet: Purim es mañana. Planifica las cuatro mitzvot: Meguilá (noche y mañana), matanot la'evyonim, mishloach manot y la seudá festiva de mañana (comida vespertina). Confirma horarios de lectura con tu shul.

es.key: Take a minute to pray for someone else's needs before your own. The sages teach that when you pray for others with pure intentions, and you happen to need the same thing, you get answered first!
es.snippet: Tómate un minuto para orar por las necesidades de otros antes que las tuyas. Los sabios enseñan que cuando oras por otros con intenciones puras y tú necesitas lo mismo, ¡te responden primero!

es.key: Aggadah is the non-legal storytelling and teaching in the Talmud and midrash — ethics, theology, and narrative. It complements halacha (law). Shabbat table stories and many midrashim are aggadah.
es.snippet: Aggadah es la narrativa y enseñanza no legal en el Talmud y midrash — ética, teología y relatos. Complementa la halajá (ley). Las historias de mesa de Shabat y muchos midrashim son aggadah.

es.key: Please put away your device and keep Shabbat. Rest, pray, learn Torah, and enjoy time with family and community. Melacha (forbidden labor) applies on Shabbat, including most phone and device use.
es.snippet: Por favor guarda tu dispositivo y guarda Shabat. Descansa, ora, aprende Torá y disfruta con familia y comunidad. Melacha (trabajo prohibido) aplica en Shabat, incluyendo la mayoría del uso de teléfono y dispositivos.

es.key: Nevi'im (Prophets) is the middle section of Tanach — Joshua, Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah, and the other prophetic books. They call Israel to justice and loyalty to Hashem. Reading them is Torah study.
es.snippet: Nevi'im (Profetas) es la sección central del Tanaj — Josué, Reyes, Isaías, Jeremías y los demás libros proféticos. Llaman a Israel a la justicia y lealtad a Hashem. Leerlos es estudio de Torá.

es.key: Sephardi siddurim open Mincha with Korbanot — especially Parshat HaTamid (the daily Temple offering) — before Ashrei and the Amidah (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Follow your siddur and kehilla.
es.snippet: Los sidurim sefardíes abren Minjá con Korbanot — especialmente Parshat HaTamid — antes de Ashrei y la Amidá (Shulján Aruj O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Siga su sidur y kehilá.

es.key: catch-all blessing on fragrant herbs, spices, flowers, fruit, etc. (often said as the spice blessing in Havdalah after Shabbat, though some will say a more specific blessing on pleasant fragrances)
es.snippet: bendición general sobre hierbas aromáticas, especias, flores, fruta, etc. (a menudo dicha como bendición de especias en Havdalah tras Shabat, aunque algunos dicen una bendición más específica sobre fragancias agradables)

es.key: kallah — Kallah means bride; kallah classes teach taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot of the home, and wedding laws before marriage. The kallah and chatan prepare for chuppah and building a Jewish family.
es.snippet: kallah — Kallah significa novia; las clases de kallah enseñan taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot del hogar y leyes de boda antes del matrimonio. La kallah y el chatan se preparan para la jupá y construir una familia judía.

es.key:
When (Northern Hemisphere — your location):
Say it during Nissan, when fruit trees in your area begin to blossom — ideally as early in the month as you first see flowers (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 226:1).
es.snippet:
Cuándo (hemisferio norte — tu ubicación):
Dilo durante Nissan, cuando los árboles frutales de tu zona empiecen a florecer — idealmente tan pronto en el mes como veas las primeras flores (Shuljan Aruj O.C. 226:1).

es.key: Nefesh is the 'animal' part of the soul closest to physical life — appetite, hunger, physical desires, etc. Torah and mitzvot attempt to refine the nefesh toward holiness instead of animal instincts.
es.snippet: Nefesh es la parte 'animal' del alma más cercana a la vida física — apetito, hambre, deseos físicos, etc. Torá y mitzvot intentan refinar el nefesh hacia la santidad en lugar de instintos animales.

es.key: Spread the joy of mitzvot! Share the Mitz Mode app with your friends 📱. Every time they use it to do a mitzvah, you get credit for that mitzvah too! It's like creating an endless chain of good deeds!
es.snippet: ¡Difunde la alegría de las mitzvot! Comparte la app Mitz Mode con tus amigos 📱. Cada vez que la usen para hacer una mitzvá, ¡tú también recibes crédito por esa mitzvá! ¡Es como crear una cadena infinita de buenas acciones!

es.key: bracha acharona — Bracha acharona is the after-blessing said when you ate or drank enough of a food but did not have a bread meal requiring Birkat Hamazon — for example borei nefashot or al hamichya.
es.snippet: bracha acharona — Bracha acharona es la bendición posterior cuando comiste o bebiste suficiente de un alimento pero no tuviste una comida con pan que requiera Birkat Hamazon — por ejemplo borei nefashot o al hamichya.

es.key: Ruach Ra'ah — Ruach Ra'ah ("evil spirit") is a rabbinic term for the impurity resting on hands after sleep. Morning netilat yadayim removes it by washing hands alternating right and left 3 times each.
es.snippet: Ruach Ra'ah — Ruach Ra'ah ("espíritu malo") es un término rabínico para la impureza en las manos tras dormir. El netilat yadayim matutino lo elimina lavando manos alternando derecha e izquierda 3 veces cada una.

es.key: Chametz is leavened grain product made from wheat, barley, rye, oats, or spelt. Bread, beer, and many processed foods can be chametz. On Pesach, owning, eating, or benefiting from chametz is forbidden.
es.snippet: Chametz es producto de grano fermentado de trigo, cebada, centeno, avena o espelta. Pan, cerveza y muchos alimentos procesados pueden ser chametz. En Pesaj, poseer, comer o beneficiarse de chametz está prohibido.

es.key: Vatodi'enu — Vatodi'enu ("You have made us know") is an insert in the Maariv Amidah on Saturday night when Yom Tov begins after Shabbat. It acknowledges that Shabbat has ended and the festival has begun.
es.snippet: Vatodi'enu — Vatodi'enu ("Nos has hecho saber") es una inserción en la Amidá de Maariv el sábado por la noche cuando Yom Tov comienza tras Shabat. Reconoce que Shabat terminó y la festividad comenzó.

es.key: Get rid of something chametz (leavened) you're not going to eat and have in mind that you're fulfilling the mitzvah of cleaning your home of chametz for Pesach. If it is currently Pesach, eat some matzah!
es.snippet: Desecha algo chametz (fermentado) que no vayas a comer y ten presente que cumples la mitzvá de limpiar tu hogar de chametz para Pesaj. Si ahora es Pesaj, ¡come un poco de matzá!

fr.key:
Chabad — Full Hallel (Chanukah):
Follow Ashkenazic custom — recite the blessings before Full Hallel (Alter Rebbe's Siddur).
fr.snippet:
Habad — Full Hallel (Hanoucca) :
Suivez la coutume ashkenaze — recitez les benedictions avant le Full Hallel (Siddour Alter Rebbe).

fr.key: Taanit Bechorot — Fast of the Firstborn on Erev Pesach (many break or nullify the fast with the seudat mitzvah after a siyum)
fr.snippet: Ta'anit Bechorot — Jeune des premiers-nes a Erev Pessah (beaucoup l'interrompent ou l'annulent avec la seudat mitzvah apres un siyoum)

fr.key: This year, Shabbat is Erev $chagName — $chagName begins tonight at nightfall (Motzei Shabbat). This doesn't happen every year.
fr.snippet: Cette année, Chabbat est la veille de $chagName — $chagName commence ce soir à la sortie de Chabbat (Motzei Chabbat). Cela n'arrive pas chaque année.

fr.key: Shamayim means Heaven or the skies — in yirat Shamayim (awe of Heaven) it means living with awareness that G-d sees all actions.
fr.snippet: Shamayim signifie Ciel ou les cieux — dans yirat Shamayim (crainte du Ciel) cela signifie vivre avec conscience que D. voit toutes les actions.

fr.key: Kiddush — Kiddush sanctifies Shabbat or Yom Tov over wine (or grape juice, or possibly bread/other drinks if in need; ask a rav).
fr.snippet: Kiddouch — Kiddouch sanctifie Chabbat ou Yom Tov sur le vin (ou jus de raisin, ou possiblement pain/autres boissons si besoin ; demandez a un rav).

fr.key: This year, Shabbat is Erev Rosh Hashana — Rosh Hashana begins tonight at nightfall (Motzei Shabbat). This doesn't happen every year.
fr.snippet: Cette année, Chabbat est la veille de Roch Hachana — Roch Hachana commence ce soir à la sortie de Chabbat. Cela n'arrive pas chaque année.

fr.key: Get a Chumash and open to a random page and verse. whatever verse you land on, it's supposed to be a message from G-d - Submitted by HB
fr.snippet: Prenez un Houmach et ouvrez a une page et un verset au hasard. Le verset sur lequel vous tombez est cense etre un message de D. — Soumis par HB

fr.key: matzah balls; those who observe the stringency of not eating gebrochts do not eat kneidlach on Pesach until the 8th day in the diaspora
fr.snippet: boulettes de matza ; ceux qui observent la stringence de ne pas manger gebrokts ne mangent pas kneidlach a Pessah jusqu'au 8e jour en diaspora

fr.key:
Chabad — Half Hallel:
Follow Ashkenazic custom — recite the blessings before Half Hallel (Alter Rebbe's Siddur; ask your rav if unsure).
fr.snippet:
Habad — Half Hallel :
Suivez la coutume ashkenaze — recitez les benedictions avant le Half Hallel (Siddour Alter Rebbe ; demandez a votre rav si incertain).

fr.key: This is the knobby, stem-like protrusion that extends from the top of the etrog (opposite the branch). The shoshanta rests on top of it.
fr.snippet: C'est la protuberance nodulaire en forme de tige qui s'etend du sommet de l'etrog (opposee a la branche). La shoshanta repose dessus.

fr.key: Shabbat candles before sunset; weekday Erev Yom Tov before sunset; Motzei Shabbat going into a Yom Tov at tzeit from a pre-existing flame
fr.snippet: Bougies de Chabbat avant le coucher du soleil ; Erev Yom Tov en semaine avant le coucher ; Motzei Chabbat entrant dans Yom Tov au tzeit d'une flamme existante

fr.snippet: periode de compte entre le 2e jour de Pessah et Chavouot ; coutumes de deuil et leur fin varient selon minhag (Ashkenaz, Sepharad, Habad, etc.)

fr.key: Mishloach manot are ready-to-eat food gifts sent to at least one Jewish friend on Purim day — minimally two different foods in one package.
fr.snippet: Mishloach manot sont des cadeaux alimentaires prets a manger envoyes a au moins un ami juif a Pourim — minimum deux aliments differents dans un colis.

fr.key: Shamayim — Shamayim means Heaven or the skies — in yirat Shamayim (awe of Heaven) it means living with awareness that G-d sees all actions.
fr.snippet: Shamayim — Shamayim signifie Ciel ou les cieux — dans yirat Shamayim cela signifie vivre avec conscience que D. voit toutes les actions.

fr.key: This checklist tries to include the basic Torah obligations and relevant information for Jewish people to live a rewarding Torah lifestyle.
fr.snippet: Cette liste vise à inclure les obligations toraniques de base et l'information pertinente pour une vie de Torah épanouie.

fr.key: Ashkenaz siddurim include Korbanot (Temple offerings) before Pesukei Dezimra. Many say at least Parshat HaTamid; follow your siddur and rabbi.
fr.snippet: Les sidourim ashkénazes incluent Korbanot avant Pessoukei Dezimra. Beaucoup disent au moins Parshat HaTamid ; suivez votre sidour et rabbin.

fr.key: Rabbi is the English word for a Jewish spiritual leader with rabbinical ordination (semicha). From Hebrew Ribbi ("my master" or "my teacher").
fr.snippet: Rabbi est le mot anglais pour un leader spirituel juif avec ordination rabbinique (semikha). De l'hebreu Ribbi ("mon maitre" ou "mon enseignant").

fr.key:
Blessings before reading:
• Al mikra megillah
• She'asa nissim
• Shehecheyanu (recited on the first night; Ashkenazim recite it by day as well)
fr.snippet:
Benedictions avant la lecture :
• Al mikra megillah
• She'asa nissim
• Shehecheyanu (recitee la premiere nuit ; Ashkenazim aussi le jour)

fr.key: This year, tomorrow (Shabbat) is Erev $chagName — $chagName begins tomorrow night at nightfall (Motzei Shabbat). This doesn't happen every year.
fr.snippet: Cette année, demain (Chabbat) est la veille de $chagName — $chagName commence demain soir à la sortie de Chabbat. Cela n'arrive pas chaque année.

fr.key: pitom — This is the knobby, stem-like protrusion that extends from the top of the etrog (opposite the branch). The shoshanta rests on top of it.
fr.snippet: pitom — C'est la protuberance nodulaire en forme de tige qui s'etend du sommet de l'etrog (opposee a la branche). La shoshanta repose dessus.

fr.key: Priestly blessing; recited daily by everyone in Israel and by Sephardim worldwide; usually recited only on festivals by Ashkenazim in the Diaspora
fr.snippet: Benediction sacerdotale ; recitee quotidiennement par tous en Israel et par les Sepharadim dans le monde ; generalement seulement aux fetes par les Ashkenazim en Diaspora

fr.key: kneidlach — matzah balls; those who observe the stringency of not eating gebrochts do not eat kneidlach on Pesach until the 8th day in the diaspora
fr.snippet: kneidlach — boulettes de matza ; ceux qui observent gebrokts ne mangent pas kneidlach a Pessah jusqu'au 8e jour en diaspora

fr.key: Sefirah — counting period between 2nd day of Pesach and Shavuot; mourning customs and when they end vary by minhag (Ashkenaz, Sephard, Chabad, etc.)
fr.snippet: Sefira — periode de compte entre le 2e jour de Pessah et Chavouot ; coutumes de deuil varient selon minhag

fr.key: 33rd day of the Omer; mourning eased in many communities; Chabad keeps haircut restrictions until Shavuot except for a 3 year old boy's first haircut
fr.snippet: 33e jour de l'Omer ; deuil allege dans de nombreuses communautes ; Habad garde restrictions de coupe jusqu'a Chavouot sauf premiere coupe a 3 ans

fr.key: Rabbi — Rabbi is the English word for a Jewish spiritual leader with rabbinical ordination (semicha). From Hebrew Ribbi ("my master" or "my teacher").
fr.snippet: Rabbi — Rabbi est le mot anglais pour un leader spirituel juif avec ordination rabbinique (semikha). De l'hebreu Ribbi ("mon maitre" ou "mon enseignant").

fr.key: This year, tomorrow (Shabbat) is Erev Rosh Hashana — Rosh Hashana begins tomorrow night at nightfall (Motzei Shabbat). This doesn't happen every year.
fr.snippet: Cette année, demain (Chabbat) est la veille de Roch Hachana — Roch Hachana commence demain soir à la sortie de Chabbat. Cela n'arrive pas chaque année.

fr.key: Shul (synagogue) is the community house of prayer, Torah, and chesed. Beyond services, it often hosts classes, youth programs, and charity organizations.
fr.snippet: Shul (synagogue) est la maison communautaire de priere, Torah et chesed. Au-dela des offices, elle accueille cours, programmes jeunesse et organisations de tsedaka.

fr.key: candle lighting — Shabbat candles before sunset; weekday Erev Yom Tov before sunset; Motzei Shabbat going into a Yom Tov at tzeit from a pre-existing flame
fr.snippet: Allumage des bougies — bougies de Chabbat avant le coucher ; Erev Yom Tov en semaine ; Motzei Chabbat entrant dans Yom Tov au tzeit d'une flamme existante

fr.key: A mechitza divides men and women's seating in shul during prayer so focus stays on tefillah per traditional practice. Heights and designs vary by community.
fr.snippet: Une mehitsa separe les places des hommes et des femmes a la shul pendant la priere pour garder le focus sur la tefila. Hauteurs et designs varient selon la communaute.

fr.key: Mishloach Manot — Mishloach manot are ready-to-eat food gifts sent to at least one Jewish friend on Purim day — minimally two different foods in one package.
fr.snippet: Mishloach Manot — cadeaux alimentaires prets a manger envoyes a au moins un ami juif a Pourim — minimum deux aliments differents dans un colis.

fr.key: shul — Shul (synagogue) is the community house of prayer, Torah, and chesed. Beyond services, it often hosts classes, youth programs, and charity organizations.
fr.snippet: shul — Shul (synagogue) est la maison communautaire de priere, Torah et chesed. Au-dela des offices, elle accueille cours, programmes jeunesse et organisations de tsedaka.

fr.key: The Beit HaMikdash (Holy Temple) stood in Jerusalem as the center of sacrifice, pilgrimage, and divine presence. Its destruction on Tisha B'Av is mourned yearly.
fr.snippet: Le Beit HaMikdash (Temple Saint) se dressait a Jerusalem comme centre de sacrifice, pelerinage et presence divine. Sa destruction a Ticha BeAv est pleuree chaque annee.

fr.key: Lag BaOmer — 33rd day of the Omer; mourning eased in many communities; Chabad keeps haircut restrictions until Shavuot except for a 3 year old boy's first haircut
fr.snippet: Lag BaOmer — 33e jour de l'Omer ; deuil allege ; Habad garde restrictions de coupe jusqu'a Chavouot sauf premiere coupe a 3 ans

fr.key: Birkat Kohanim — Priestly blessing; recited daily by everyone in Israel and by Sephardim worldwide; usually recited only on festivals by Ashkenazim in the Diaspora
fr.snippet: Birkat Kohanim — Benediction sacerdotale ; recitee quotidiennement par tous en Israel et par les Sepharadim ; generalement seulement aux fetes par les Ashkenazim en Diaspora

fr.key: Say/text something nice to someone else or do a nice act for them. An act of kindness with no ulterior motives (except for scoring yet another mitzvah! Cha-Ching!)
fr.snippet: Dites ou envoyez quelque chose d'agreable a quelqu'un ou faites un acte gentil. Un acte de bonte sans arriere-pensee (sauf une mitzvah de plus ! Cha-Ching !)

fr.key:
Sephardi — Half Hallel:
Do not recite a blessing over Partial (Half) Hallel — say the psalms without a bracha (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 422:2; Peninei Halakha 05-01-12).
fr.snippet:
Sepharadi — Half Hallel :
Ne recitez pas de benediction sur le Half Hallel — dites les psaumes sans bracha (Choulhan Aroukh O.C. 422:2 ; Peninei Halakha 05-01-12).

fr.key: a group of Jews who follow a movement that emphasizes deep mystical devotion, joyful worship, and a close-knit community led by a spiritual leader known as a Rebbe.
fr.snippet: groupe de Juifs suivant un mouvement qui met l'accent sur la devotion mystique, le culte joyeux et une communaute unie dirigee par un Rebbe

fr.key: Today is $holidayName (Yom Tov — a festival day). Please put away your device and keep the day holy. Melacha (forbidden labor) applies on Yom Tov similar to Shabbat.
fr.snippet: Aujourd'hui c'est $holidayName (Yom Tov — jour de fete). Veuillez ranger votre appareil et garder le jour saint. Melacha s'applique le Yom Tov comme Chabbat.

fr.snippet: mechitza — Une mechitza sépare les places des hommes et des femmes à la shul pendant la prière afin que l'attention reste sur la tefillah selon la pratique traditionnelle. Hauteurs et designs varient selon la communauté.

fr.key: Shehecheyanu (Ashkenaz — Rema O.C. 692:1): Recite before the first evening Megillah reading. Ashkenazim also recite Shehecheyanu before the first daytime Megillah reading.
fr.snippet: Shehecheyanu (Ashkenaz — Rema O.C. 692:1) : Réciter avant la première lecture du soir de la Meguila. Les Ashkenazim récitent aussi Shehecheyanu avant la première lecture de jour de la Meguila.

fr.key: Chasidim — a group of Jews who follow a movement that emphasizes deep mystical devotion, joyful worship, and a close-knit community led by a spiritual leader known as a Rebbe.
fr.snippet: Hassidim — groupe de Juifs suivant un mouvement qui met l'accent sur la dévotion mystique profonde, le culte joyeux et une communauté soudée dirigée par un Rebbe.

fr.key: Sephardi and Edot HaMizrach communities in the Diaspora commonly recite Birkat Kohanim daily during Shacharit (customs vary — follow your kehilla and Shulchan Aruch O.C. 128).
fr.snippet: Les communautés séfarades et Edot HaMizrah en Diaspora récitent souvent Birkat Kohanim chaque jour à Shaharit (coutumes variables — suivez votre kehilla et Choulhan Aroukh O.C. 128).

fr.key: Beit HaMikdash — The Beit HaMikdash (Holy Temple) stood in Jerusalem as the center of sacrifice, pilgrimage, and divine presence. Its destruction on Tisha B'Av is mourned yearly.
fr.snippet: Beit HaMikdash — Le Beit HaMikdash (Temple Saint) se dressait à Jérusalem comme centre de sacrifice, pèlerinage et présence divine. Sa destruction à Ticha BeAv est pleurée chaque année.

fr.key: Bracha acharona is the after-blessing said when you ate or drank enough of a food but did not have a bread meal requiring Birkat Hamazon — for example borei nefashot or al hamichya.
fr.snippet: Bracha acharona est la bénédiction après avoir mangé ou bu assez d'un aliment sans repas de pain nécessitant Birkat HaMazon — par exemple borei nefashot ou al hamichya.

fr.key: YaKNeHaZ is a mnemonic for the order when Shabbat flows directly into Yom Tov (for example Saturday night Pesach). Use your siddur's festival night Kiddush section for instructions.
fr.snippet: YaKNeHaZ est un moyen mnémotechnique pour l'ordre quand Chabbat enchaîne directement sur Yom Tov (par ex. samedi soir de Pessah). Consultez la section Kiddush de fête du soir de votre siddour.

fr.key: Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) is the blessing on grain foods that are not bread — cake, crackers, pasta, cereal. It is shorter than hamotzi but still thanks G-d for grain sustenance.
fr.snippet: Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) est la bénédiction sur les aliments de céréales qui ne sont pas du pain — gâteau, crackers, pâtes, céréales. Plus courte que hamotzi mais remercie D. pour la subsistance de céréales.

fr.key: Next time you have guests, keep in mind to escort them on their way as they leave, even for just a few steps. The Talmud (Sotah 46b) says the reward for escorting others has no measure!
fr.snippet: La prochaine fois que vous avez des invités, pensez à les accompagner en partant, même quelques pas. Le Talmud (Sotah 46b) dit que la récompense de l'accompagnement n'a pas de mesure !

fr.key: Ruach Ra'ah ("evil spirit") is a rabbinic term for the impurity resting on hands after sleep. Morning netilat yadayim removes it by washing hands alternating right and left 3 times each.
fr.snippet: Ruach Ra'ah (« esprit mauvais ») est un terme rabbinique pour l'impureté sur les mains après le sommeil. Le netilat yadayim du matin l'enlève en lavant les mains droite et gauche 3 fois chacune.

fr.key: Asara B'Tevet — minor fast on 10 Tevet from dawn until nightfall; the only fast never postponed when it falls on Friday before Shabbat — in which case we break it with Friday night Kiddush
fr.snippet: Asara B'Tevet — jeûne mineur le 10 Tévet de l'aube à la nuit ; le seul jeûne jamais reporté quand il tombe un vendredi avant Chabbat — auquel cas on le rompt avec le Kiddush du vendredi soir

fr.key: Kallah means bride; kallah classes teach taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot of the home, and wedding laws before marriage. The kallah and chatan prepare for chuppah and building a Jewish family.
fr.snippet: Kallah signifie mariée ; les cours de kallah enseignent taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot du foyer et lois de mariage avant les noces. La kallah et le hatan se préparent pour la houppa et bâtir une famille juive.

fr.key: Vatodi'enu ("You have made us know") is an insert in the Maariv Amidah on Saturday night when Yom Tov begins after Shabbat. It acknowledges that Shabbat has ended and the festival has begun.
fr.snippet: Vatodi'enu (« Tu nous as fait savoir ») est une insertion dans l'Amidah de Maariv le samedi soir quand Yom Tov commence après Chabbat. Elle reconnaît que Chabbat est fini et la fête a commencé.

fr.key: Mezonot — Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) is the blessing on grain foods that are not bread — cake, crackers, pasta, cereal. It is shorter than hamotzi but still thanks G-d for grain sustenance.
fr.snippet: Mezonot — Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) est la bénédiction sur les aliments de céréales qui ne sont pas du pain — gâteau, crackers, pâtes, céréales. Plus courte que hamotzi mais remercie D. pour la subsistance de céréales.

fr.key: Yaknehaz — YaKNeHaZ is a mnemonic for the order when Shabbat flows directly into Yom Tov (for example Saturday night Pesach). Use your siddur's festival night Kiddush section for instructions.
fr.snippet: Yaknehaz — YaKNeHaZ est un moyen mnémotechnique pour l'ordre quand Chabbat enchaîne directement sur Yom Tov (par ex. samedi soir de Pessah). Consultez la section Kiddush de fête du soir de votre siddour.

fr.key: Purim is tomorrow. Plan all four mitzvot: Megillah (night and morning), matanot la'evyonim, mishloach manot, and tomorrow's festive seudah (afternoon meal). Confirm reading times with your shul.
fr.snippet: Pourim est demain. Planifiez les quatre mitzvot : Meguila (soir et matin), matanot la'evyonim, mishloach manot et la seudah festive de demain (repas de l'après-midi). Confirmez les horaires de lecture avec votre shul.

fr.key: Take a minute to pray for someone else's needs before your own. The sages teach that when you pray for others with pure intentions, and you happen to need the same thing, you get answered first!
fr.snippet: Prenez une minute pour prier pour les besoins d'autrui avant les vôtres. Les sages enseignent que quand vous priez pour les autres avec de bonnes intentions et que vous avez le même besoin, vous êtes exaucé en premier !

fr.key: Aggadah is the non-legal storytelling and teaching in the Talmud and midrash — ethics, theology, and narrative. It complements halacha (law). Shabbat table stories and many midrashim are aggadah.
fr.snippet: Aggadah est le récit et l'enseignement non juridique du Talmud et du midrash — éthique, théologie et narration. Elle complète la halakha (loi). Les histoires de table de Chabbat et beaucoup de midrashim sont aggadah.

fr.key: Please put away your device and keep Shabbat. Rest, pray, learn Torah, and enjoy time with family and community. Melacha (forbidden labor) applies on Shabbat, including most phone and device use.
fr.snippet: Veuillez ranger votre appareil et garder Chabbat. Reposez-vous, priez, étudiez la Torah et profitez de la famille et de la communauté. Melacha (travail interdit) s'applique le Chabbat, y compris la plupart de l'usage du téléphone et des app

fr.key: Nevi'im (Prophets) is the middle section of Tanach — Joshua, Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah, and the other prophetic books. They call Israel to justice and loyalty to Hashem. Reading them is Torah study.
fr.snippet: Nevi'im (Prophètes) est la section centrale du Tanakh — Josué, Rois, Isaïe, Jérémie et les autres livres prophétiques. Ils appellent Israël à la justice et à la fidélité à Hachem. Les lire est étude de Torah.

fr.key: Sephardi siddurim open Mincha with Korbanot — especially Parshat HaTamid (the daily Temple offering) — before Ashrei and the Amidah (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Follow your siddur and kehilla.
fr.snippet: Les sidourim séfarades ouvrent Min'ha par Korbanot — surtout Parshat HaTamid — avant Ashrei et l'Amidah (Choulhan Aroukh O.C. 1:5, 232:1). Suivez votre sidour et kehilla.

fr.key: catch-all blessing on fragrant herbs, spices, flowers, fruit, etc. (often said as the spice blessing in Havdalah after Shabbat, though some will say a more specific blessing on pleasant fragrances)
fr.snippet: bénédiction générale sur herbes parfumées, épices, fleurs, fruits, etc. (souvent dite comme bénédiction des épices à Havdalah après Chabbat, bien que certains disent une bénédiction plus spécifique sur les parfums agréables)

fr.key: kallah — Kallah means bride; kallah classes teach taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot of the home, and wedding laws before marriage. The kallah and chatan prepare for chuppah and building a Jewish family.
fr.snippet: kallah — Kallah signifie mariée ; les cours de kallah enseignent taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot du foyer et lois de mariage avant les noces. La kallah et le hatan se préparent pour la houppa et bâtir une famille juive.

fr.key:
When (Northern Hemisphere — your location):
Say it during Nissan, when fruit trees in your area begin to blossom — ideally as early in the month as you first see flowers (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 226:1).
fr.snippet:
Quand (hémisphère nord — votre lieu) :
Dites-le pendant Nissan, quand les arbres fruitiers de votre région commencent à fleurir — idéalement dès les premières fleurs du mois (Choulhan Aroukh O.C. 226:1).

fr.key: Nefesh is the 'animal' part of the soul closest to physical life — appetite, hunger, physical desires, etc. Torah and mitzvot attempt to refine the nefesh toward holiness instead of animal instincts.
fr.snippet: Nefesh est la partie « animale » de l'âme la plus proche de la vie physique — appétit, faim, désirs physiques, etc. Torah et mitzvot cherchent à raffiner le nefesh vers la sainteté plutôt que les instincts animaux.

fr.key: Spread the joy of mitzvot! Share the Mitz Mode app with your friends 📱. Every time they use it to do a mitzvah, you get credit for that mitzvah too! It's like creating an endless chain of good deeds!
fr.snippet: Diffusez la joie des mitzvot ! Partagez l'app Mitz Mode avec vos amis 📱. Chaque fois qu'ils l'utilisent pour une mitzvah, vous recevez aussi le mérite de cette mitzvah ! C'est comme créer une chaîne infinie de bonnes actions !

fr.key: Chametz is leavened grain product made from wheat, barley, rye, oats, or spelt. Bread, beer, and many processed foods can be chametz. On Pesach, owning, eating, or benefiting from chametz is forbidden.
fr.snippet: Chametz est un produit de céréales levé de blé, orge, seigle, avoine ou épeautre. Pain, bière et beaucoup d'aliments transformés peuvent être chametz. À Pessah, posséder, manger ou profiter de chametz est interdit.

fr.key: Vatodi'enu — Vatodi'enu ("You have made us know") is an insert in the Maariv Amidah on Saturday night when Yom Tov begins after Shabbat. It acknowledges that Shabbat has ended and the festival has begun.
fr.snippet: Vatodi'enu — Vatodi'enu (« Tu nous as fait savoir ») est une insertion dans l'Amidah de Maariv le samedi soir quand Yom Tov commence après Chabbat. Elle reconnaît que Chabbat est fini et la fête a commencé.

fr.key: Get rid of something chametz (leavened) you're not going to eat and have in mind that you're fulfilling the mitzvah of cleaning your home of chametz for Pesach. If it is currently Pesach, eat some matzah!
fr.snippet: Débarrassez-vous d'un chametz (levé) que vous ne mangerez pas en ayant l'intention d'accomplir la mitzvah de nettoyer votre maison du chametz pour Pessah. Si nous sommes déjà à Pessah, mangez de la matza !

ru.key:
Chabad — Full Hallel (Chanukah):
Follow Ashkenazic custom — recite the blessings before Full Hallel (Alter Rebbe's Siddur).
ru.snippet:
Chabad — Full Hallel (Hanukah):
Sleduyte ashkenazskomu obychayu — proiznesite blagosloveniya pered Full Hallel (Siddur Alter Rebbe).

ru.key: Taanit Bechorot — Fast of the Firstborn on Erev Pesach (many break or nullify the fast with the seudat mitzvah after a siyum)
ru.snippet: Taanit Bechorot — Post pervorodnykh v Erev Pesach (mnogie preryvayut post seudat mitzvah posle siyum)

ru.key: Shamayim means Heaven or the skies — in yirat Shamayim (awe of Heaven) it means living with awareness that G-d sees all actions.
ru.snippet: Shamayim oznachaet Nebo ili nebesa — v yirat Shamayim eto zhizn s osoznaniem, chto V-gh vidit vse postupki.

ru.key: Kiddush — Kiddush sanctifies Shabbat or Yom Tov over wine (or grape juice, or possibly bread/other drinks if in need; ask a rav).
ru.snippet: Kiddush — Kiddush osvyashchaet Shabbat ili Yom Tov nad vinom (ili vinogradnym sokom; sprosite rava pri nuzhde).

ru.key: Get a Chumash and open to a random page and verse. whatever verse you land on, it's supposed to be a message from G-d - Submitted by HB
ru.snippet: Voz'mite Humash i otkroyte na sluchaynoy stranitse i stikhe. Stikh, na kotoryy popadete, dolzhen byt poslaniyem ot V-ga — Ot HB

ru.key: matzah balls; those who observe the stringency of not eating gebrochts do not eat kneidlach on Pesach until the 8th day in the diaspora
ru.snippet: matzah-klyotsy; soblyudayushchie strogoct ne yest gebrokts ne yedyat kneidlach na Pesach do 8-go dnya v galute

ru.key:
Chabad — Half Hallel:
Follow Ashkenazic custom — recite the blessings before Half Hallel (Alter Rebbe's Siddur; ask your rav if unsure).
ru.snippet:
Chabad — Half Hallel:
Sleduyte ashkenazskomu obychayu — proiznesite blagosloveniya pered Half Hallel (Siddur Alter Rebbe; sprosite rava esli ne uvereny).

ru.key: This is the knobby, stem-like protrusion that extends from the top of the etrog (opposite the branch). The shoshanta rests on top of it.
ru.snippet: Eto bugraatoe steblevidnoe vystuplenie na vershine etrog (protiv vetki). Shoshanta pokoyetsya na nem.

ru.snippet: Shabbat-svechi do zakata; budniy Erev Yom Tov do zakata; Motzei Shabbat v Yom Tov v tzeit s suschestvuyushchego ognya

ru.key: counting period between 2nd day of Pesach and Shavuot; mourning customs and when they end vary by minhag (Ashkenaz, Sephard, Chabad, etc.)
ru.snippet: period scheta mezhdu 2-m dnyom Pesach i Shavuot; obychai skorbi i i okonchanie razlichayutsya po minhag (Ashkenaz, Sephard, Chabad i t.d.)

ru.key: Mishloach manot are ready-to-eat food gifts sent to at least one Jewish friend on Purim day — minimally two different foods in one package.
ru.snippet: Mishloach manot — gotovye edible podarki khotya by odnomu evreyskomu drugu v Purim — minimum dva raznykh produkta v odnoy upakovke.

ru.snippet: Rabbi — angliyskoe slovo dlya evreyskogo dukhovnogo lidera s semicha. Ot ivrita Ribbi ("moy uchitel").

ru.key:
Blessings before reading:
• Al mikra megillah
• She'asa nissim
• Shehecheyanu (recited on the first night; Ashkenazim recite it by day as well)
ru.snippet:
Blagosloveniya pered chteniem:
• Al mikra megillah
• She'asa nissim
• Shehecheyanu (v pervyy vecher; ashkenazy takzhe dnem)

ru.key: Say/text something nice to someone else or do a nice act for them. An act of kindness with no ulterior motives (except for scoring yet another mitzvah! Cha-Ching!)
ru.snippet: Skazhite ili otpravte chto-to priyatnoe drugomu ili sdelayte dobroe delo. Akt dobroty bez skrytykh motivev (krome esche odной mitzvah! Cha-Ching!)

ru.key:
Sephardi — Half Hallel:
Do not recite a blessing over Partial (Half) Hallel — say the psalms without a bracha (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 422:2; Peninei Halakha 05-01-12).
ru.snippet:
Sephardi — Half Hallel:
Ne proiznosite brakhu nad Half Hallel — chitayte psalmy bez brakhi (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 422:2; Peninei Halakha 05-01-12).

ru.key: Today is $holidayName (Yom Tov — a festival day). Please put away your device and keep the day holy. Melacha (forbidden labor) applies on Yom Tov similar to Shabbat.
ru.snippet: Segodnya $holidayName (Yom Tov — prazdnichnyy den). Uберите устройство и соблюдайте святость дня. Melacha применяется в Yom Tov как в Shabbat.

ru.key: Shehecheyanu (Ashkenaz — Rema O.C. 692:1): Recite before the first evening Megillah reading. Ashkenazim also recite Shehecheyanu before the first daytime Megillah reading.
ru.snippet: Shehecheyanu (Ashkenaz — Rema O.C. 692:1): Proiznosite pered pervym vechernim chteniem Megillah. Ashkenazy takzhe proiznosyat Shehecheyanu pered pervym dnevnym chteniem Megillah.

ru.key: Saw a lost object recently? 🔍 Don't just ignore it! Go back and try to return it to its owner (if possible). That's the mitzvah of Hashavat Aveidah (returning lost objects).
ru.snippet: Nedavno videli poteryannuyu veshch? 🔍 Ne ignoriruyte! Vernites i poprobuyte vernut ee vladeltsu (esli vozmozhno). Eto mitzvah Hashavat Aveidah (vozvrashchenie poteryannogo).

ru.key: Chasidim — a group of Jews who follow a movement that emphasizes deep mystical devotion, joyful worship, and a close-knit community led by a spiritual leader known as a Rebbe.
ru.snippet: Hasidim — gruppa evreev, sleduyushchikh dvizheniyu s glubokoy mistической predannostyu, radostnym bogosluzheniem i tesnoy obshchinoy pod rukovodstvom Rebbe.

ru.key: Beit HaMikdash — The Beit HaMikdash (Holy Temple) stood in Jerusalem as the center of sacrifice, pilgrimage, and divine presence. Its destruction on Tisha B'Av is mourned yearly.
ru.snippet: Beit HaMikdash — Beit HaMikdash (Svyatoy Khram) stoyal v Ierusalime kak tsentr zhertvoprinosheniy, palomnichestva i bozhestvennogo prisutstviya. Ego razrushenie v Tisha B'Av opлакивается ежегодно.

ru.key: Bracha acharona is the after-blessing said when you ate or drank enough of a food but did not have a bread meal requiring Birkat Hamazon — for example borei nefashot or al hamichya.
ru.snippet: Bracha acharona — poslebrachie posle edy ili napitka bez khlebной трапезы, trebuyushchey Birkat Hamazon — naprimer borei nefashot ili al hamichya.

ru.key: YaKNeHaZ is a mnemonic for the order when Shabbat flows directly into Yom Tov (for example Saturday night Pesach). Use your siddur's festival night Kiddush section for instructions.
ru.snippet: YaKNeHaZ — mnemonika poryadka, kogda Shabbat perekhodit neposredstvenno v Yom Tov (naprimer, subbotu vecherom Pesach). Ispolzuyte razdel prazdnichnogo vechernего Kiddush v siddure.

ru.key: Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) is the blessing on grain foods that are not bread — cake, crackers, pasta, cereal. It is shorter than hamotzi but still thanks G-d for grain sustenance.
ru.snippet: Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) — brakha na zernovye produkty, ne yavlyayushchiesya khlebom — keks, krekery, makaron, khlopya. Koroche hamotzi, no blagodarit V-ga za zernovoe propitanie.

ru.key: Next time you have guests, keep in mind to escort them on their way as they leave, even for just a few steps. The Talmud (Sotah 46b) says the reward for escorting others has no measure!
ru.snippet: V sleduyushchiy raz, kogda u vas gosti, provozhayte ikh pri ukhode, khotya by neskolko shagov. Talmud (Sotah 46b) govorit, chto nagrada za provody ne imeet predela!

ru.key: Ruach Ra'ah ("evil spirit") is a rabbinic term for the impurity resting on hands after sleep. Morning netilat yadayim removes it by washing hands alternating right and left 3 times each.
ru.snippet: Ruach Ra'ah («zloy dukh») — rabbinicheskiy termin dlya tumah na rukakh posle sna. Utrennyaya netilat yadayim ustraняet ego, promyvaya ruki po ocheredi pravyyu i levuyu po 3 raza.

ru.key: Asara B'Tevet — minor fast on 10 Tevet from dawn until nightfall; the only fast never postponed when it falls on Friday before Shabbat — in which case we break it with Friday night Kiddush
ru.snippet: Asara B'Tevet — malyy post 10 Tevet s rassveta do nochi; edinstvennyy post, kotoryy nikogda ne perenositsya, esli padaet v pyatnitsu pered Shabbat — v etom sluchae preryvaem ego pyatnichnym vechernim Kiddush

ru.key: Kallah means bride; kallah classes teach taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot of the home, and wedding laws before marriage. The kallah and chatan prepare for chuppah and building a Jewish family.
ru.snippet: Kallah oznachaet nevestu; zanyatiya kallah uchат taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot doma i svadebnym zakonam do braka. Kallah i chatan gotovyatsya k chuppah i sozdaniyu evreyskoy semyi.

ru.key: Vatodi'enu ("You have made us know") is an insert in the Maariv Amidah on Saturday night when Yom Tov begins after Shabbat. It acknowledges that Shabbat has ended and the festival has begun.
ru.snippet: Vatodi'enu («Ty sdelal nas znaющimi») — vstavka v Amidah Maariv v subbotu vecherom, kogda Yom Tov nachinaetsya posle Shabbat. Priznaet okonchanie Shabbat i nachalo prazdnika.

ru.key: Mezonot — Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) is the blessing on grain foods that are not bread — cake, crackers, pasta, cereal. It is shorter than hamotzi but still thanks G-d for grain sustenance.
ru.snippet: Mezonot — Mezonot (borei minei mezonot) — brakha na zernovye produkty, ne yavlyayushchiesya khlebom — keks, krekery, makaron, khlopya. Koroche hamotzi, no blagodarit V-ga za zernovoe propitanie.

ru.key: Yaknehaz — YaKNeHaZ is a mnemonic for the order when Shabbat flows directly into Yom Tov (for example Saturday night Pesach). Use your siddur's festival night Kiddush section for instructions.
ru.snippet: Yaknehaz — YaKNeHaZ — mnemonika poryadka, kogda Shabbat perekhodit neposredstvenno v Yom Tov (naprimer, subbotu vecherom Pesach). Ispolzuyte razdel prazdnichnogo vechernего Kiddush v siddure.

ru.key: Purim is tomorrow. Plan all four mitzvot: Megillah (night and morning), matanot la'evyonim, mishloach manot, and tomorrow's festive seudah (afternoon meal). Confirm reading times with your shul.
ru.snippet: Purim zavtra. Zaplaniruyte chetyre mitzvot: Megillah (noch i utro), matanot la'evyonim, mishloach manot i prazdnichnuyu seudah zavtra (obed posle poludnya). Utochnite vremya chteniya v shul.

ru.key: Take a minute to pray for someone else's needs before your own. The sages teach that when you pray for others with pure intentions, and you happen to need the same thing, you get answered first!
ru.snippet: Udelyte minutu molitve o nuzhdakh drugikh prezhde svoikh. Mudretsy uchат: kogda molites za drugikh s chistymi namereniyami i vam nuzhno to zhe — vam otvechayut pervym!

ru.key: Aggadah is the non-legal storytelling and teaching in the Talmud and midrash — ethics, theology, and narrative. It complements halacha (law). Shabbat table stories and many midrashim are aggadah.
ru.snippet: Aggadah — nelegalnye rasskazy i ucheniya v Talmude i midrashe — etika, bogoslovie i narrativ. Dopolnayet halakhu (zakon). Shabbat-stol rasskazy i mnogie midrashim — eto aggadah.

ru.key: Please put away your device and keep Shabbat. Rest, pray, learn Torah, and enjoy time with family and community. Melacha (forbidden labor) applies on Shabbat, including most phone and device use.
ru.snippet: Uберите ustroystvo i soblyudayte Shabbat. Otdykhnite, molites, uchites Toru i provodite vremya s semyey i obshchinoy. Melacha (zapreshchennyy trud) primenyaetsya v Shabbat, vklyuchaya bolshinstvo ispolzovaniya telefona i ustroystv.

ru.key: Nevi'im (Prophets) is the middle section of Tanach — Joshua, Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah, and the other prophetic books. They call Israel to justice and loyalty to Hashem. Reading them is Torah study.
ru.snippet: Nevi'im (Proroki) — srednyaya chast Tanakh — Yehoshua, Melakhim, Yeshayahu, Yirmiyahu i drugie knigi prorokov. Prizyvayut Izrail k spravedlivosti i vernosti Hashemu. Chtenie ikh — izuchenie Tory.

ru.key: catch-all blessing on fragrant herbs, spices, flowers, fruit, etc. (often said as the spice blessing in Havdalah after Shabbat, though some will say a more specific blessing on pleasant fragrances)
ru.snippet: obshchaya brakha na aromatnye travy, specii, tsvety, plody i t.d. (chasto kak brakha na specii v Havdalah posle Shabbat, khotya nekotorye govoryat bolee konkretnuyu brakhu na priyatnye zapakhi)

ru.key: kallah — Kallah means bride; kallah classes teach taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot of the home, and wedding laws before marriage. The kallah and chatan prepare for chuppah and building a Jewish family.
ru.snippet: kallah — Kallah oznachaet nevestu; zanyatiya kallah uchат taharat hamishpacha, mitzvot doma i svadebnym zakonam do braka. Kallah i chatan gotovyatsya k chuppah i sozdaniyu evreyskoy semyi.

ru.key:
When (Northern Hemisphere — your location):
Say it during Nissan, when fruit trees in your area begin to blossom — ideally as early in the month as you first see flowers (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 226:1).
ru.snippet:
Kogda (severnoe polusharie — vash region):
Govorite v Nissan, kogda plodovye derevya v vashem rayone nachinayut tsvet — idealno kak mozhno ranshe v mesyatse, kogda uvidite pervye tsvety (Shulchan Arukh O.C. 226:1).

ru.key: Nefesh is the 'animal' part of the soul closest to physical life — appetite, hunger, physical desires, etc. Torah and mitzvot attempt to refine the nefesh toward holiness instead of animal instincts.
ru.snippet: Nefesh — «zhivotnaya» chast dushi, blizhayshaya k fizicheskoy zhizni — appetit, golod, fizicheskie zhelaniya i t.d. Tora i mitzvot stremyatsya ochistit nefesh k kedushah vmesto zhivotnykh instinktov.

ru.key: Spread the joy of mitzvot! Share the Mitz Mode app with your friends 📱. Every time they use it to do a mitzvah, you get credit for that mitzvah too! It's like creating an endless chain of good deeds!
ru.snippet: Rasprostranyayte radost mitzvot! Podelites prilozheniem Mitz Mode s druzyami 📱. Kazhdyy raz, kogda oni delayut mitzvah cherez nego, vy tozhe poluchаете zaslugu za etu mitzvah! Eto kak beskonechnaya tsep dobrodelaniy!

ru.key: Chametz is leavened grain product made from wheat, barley, rye, oats, or spelt. Bread, beer, and many processed foods can be chametz. On Pesach, owning, eating, or benefiting from chametz is forbidden.
ru.snippet: Chametz — kislyy zernovoy produkt iz pshenitsy, yachmenya, rzhi, ovsa ili polby. Khleb, pivo i mnogie obrabotannye produkty mogut byt chametz. Na Pesach vladenie, eda ili polza ot chametz zapreshcheny.

ru.key: Get rid of something chametz (leavened) you're not going to eat and have in mind that you're fulfilling the mitzvah of cleaning your home of chametz for Pesach. If it is currently Pesach, eat some matzah!
ru.snippet: Izavites ot chem-to chametz (kislogo), chto ne budete est, s namereniem vypolnit mitzvah ochistki doma ot chametz k Pesach. Esli seychas Pesach — seshite nemnogo matzah!

ru.key: Thank G-d for giving you the ability to feel textures and temperatures 🤚. From soft bristly grass to cooling water to a fluffy blanket - every sensation is a unique blessing that adds richness to our lives!
ru.snippet: Blagodarite V-ga za sposobnost chuvstvovat faktury i temperatury 🤚. Ot myagkoy kolюchey travy do prokhladnoy vody i pushenogo odeyala — kazhdoe oshchushchenie unikalnaya brakha, obogashchayushchaya zhizn!

ru.key: Learn about Tefillin! 👑 Did you know? The head Tefillin contains the same four Torah passages as the arm Tefillin, but arranged differently! Today's mission: Learn about how Tefillin connects our minds to G-d.
ru.snippet: Uchites o Tefillin! 👑 Znali li? Tefillin na golove soderzhit te zhe chetyre otryvka Tory, chto i na ruke, no v drugom poryadke! Zadacha dnya: kak Tefillin svyazyvaet um s V-gom.

ru.key: Discover the deeper meaning of the beard in Judaism! 🧔 Did you know? Kabbalists say that the 13 attributes of Divine mercy can emanate through the hairs of the beard! Now that's what I call a valuable hairloom.
ru.snippet: Otkroyte glubokiy smysl borody v iudaizme! 🧔 Kabbалisty govoryat, chto 13 svoystv bozhestvennoy milosti mogut izluchatsya cherez volosy borody! Vot eto tsennoye nasledie!

ru.key: Maleh lugmov is drinking a cheekful from the Kiddush cup (approx 2 fl oz) after the blessing — from at least a total of a revi'it of wine or grape juice in the cup. Grape juice counts like wine for many poskim.
ru.snippet: Maleh lugmov — vypit polnye shcheki iz Kiddush-kubka (okolo 60 ml) posle brakhi — iz ne menee revi'it vina ili vinogradnogo soka. Vinogradnyy sok schitaetsya vinom u mnogikh poskim.

ru.key:
Rosh Chodesh during Chanukah — if you recite Hallel, say Full Hallel at Shacharit (the Chanukah practice), not Half Hallel (Peninei Halakha 12-02-07). Optional for women — follow your minhag.

Tachanun is omitted.
ru.snippet:
Rosh Chodesh vo vremya Chanukah — esli chitaete Hallel, govorete Polnyy Hallel na Shacharit (obychay Chanukah), ne Polu-Hallel (Peninei Halakha 12-02-07). Po zhelaniyu dlya zhenshchin — po vashemu minhag.

Tachanun propuskayetsya.

ru.key: Thank G-d for giving you the ability to smell your surroundings. Not only do you know when someone is baking cookies, but it can help you avoid danger. What if everything in the whole world smelled like cardboard?
ru.snippet: Blagodarite V-ga za sposobnost chuvstvovat zapakhi vokrug. Vy ne tolko znaete, kogda pekut pechene, no eto pomogaet izbezhat opasnosti. Chto esli by vsyo v mire pakhlo kartonom?
